items.description: Numeral '62' written in dark ink at the top left corner above the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Early foliation or pagination mark, added to organize the codex. Such numbers are typical in the Codex Regius and related Icelandic manuscripts for reference and collation.

items.description: Large Gothic capital 'S' with bold curved upper lobe and elongated descending stroke that extends prominently into the left margin.
items.scholarly_note: Enlarged initial marking the start of a new stanza or speech (text continues 'ig...' i.e. 'Sig...') in Helreið Brynhildar. Standard 13th-century Icelandic scribal practice in Codex Regius for structuring Eddic verse; the form and placement are consistent with the main scribe's hand.

items.description: Small, roughly circular dark ink blot at the right edge of the text block, approximately midway down the page.
items.scholarly_note: Accidental ink drop or localized stain from the scribe's iron-gall ink. It does not obscure letters and shows no signs of deliberate correction, erasure, or cancellation.

items.description: Faint modern inscription reading approximately '171 L. 5.' written in a later hand below the final line of medieval text.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval annotation, likely a cataloguing, shelfmark, or line-reference note added during rebinding, institutional ownership, or modern cataloguing (Royal Library of Copenhagen or Árni Magnússon Institute period).

scratches: No distinct linear scratches, drypoint marks, or incisions visible on the writing surface.
damage_notes: Typical age-related discoloration and foxing concentrated along the outer edges and lower portion of the parchment.
Minor surface wear and slight staining at the lower right corner; the leaf is intact with no holes, tears, or repairs affecting the text area.
summary: Page 71 of Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) contains text from Helreið Brynhildar / Sigurðarkviða III in a clean, regular single-column layout by the main scribe. Visible non-textual features are limited to the early numeral '62' in the upper margin, a large decorative initial 'S' protruding from the left margin to mark a textual division, one small accidental ink stain inside the text block on the right, and a faint modern cataloguing annotation at the foot of the page. The parchment exhibits only the expected mild edge discoloration and wear of a late-13th-century Icelandic vellum leaf. There are no figurative doodles, faces, animals, pen trials, interlinear corrections, offset ink, or significant marginalia.
